af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Matrix Protocol Client-Server API for federated, E2EE messaging. Access token obtained via username/password login exchange — not the most agent-friendly (no service account). E2EE requires device key management — bots in encrypted rooms must handle crypto. Federation adds routing complexity through multiple homeservers. Long-polling /sync endpoint is primary event delivery — not push-based. Bot users need appropriate power levels in rooms. Room and user IDs include homeserver domain (!room:example.com, @user:example.com). Good for privacy-first messaging bots. Retry guidance documented. GDPR compliance.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. GDPR compliant. Access token from login exchange — no OAuth, no service accounts. Tokens tied to device IDs. E2EE is a core security feature but adds implementation complexity for agent bots. No fine-grained token scoping — room power levels provide coarse access control. Matrix.org maintained (open source FOSS). Federation means messages may route through third-party homeservers — data sovereignty requires self-hosted. Access tokens via environment variables. Synapse rate limiting settings need tuning for bot use.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted Synapse or matrix.org — managed homeservers have SLA; self-hosted does not. Stable versioned spec (MSC process) with good backward compatibility. Federation compatibility maintained across spec versions. Retry guidance documented. Long-polling /sync requires resilient loop implementation. Partial idempotency — message events are not idempotent (retrying sends duplicates). Token pagination for sync. Synapse rate limits must be tuned for bot traffic patterns.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Mattermost open-source team collaboration REST API. Excellent docs and OpenAPI spec. Personal access tokens, OAuth2 with scopes, and dedicated bot account tokens. Slash command responses must reply within 3 seconds or timeout error is shown — critical for agent bots. WebSocket API for real-time events is separate from REST. Team ID required for many operations — must resolve team name to ID first. Interactive messages require response endpoint. Good for compliance-sensitive self-hosted deployments. Retry guidance documented. SOC2/HIPAA/ISO27001.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. GDPR/HIPAA/SOC2/ISO27001 compliance — strong enterprise posture. Personal access tokens, OAuth2 with scopes, dedicated bot accounts — good security model. OAuth2 scopes provide read/write separation. Bot accounts are isolated from user accounts — good least-privilege. Tokens via environment variables. System/team admin roles provide coarse RBAC. Mattermost Inc maintained. Self-hosted means data stays on-premises — critical for compliance deployments. Webhook signature verification for incoming webhooks.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA unless enterprise/Mattermost Cloud (which has SLA). Stable versioned REST API maintained via OpenAPI spec. Good migration guides across major versions. Retry guidance documented. Slash command 3-second timeout is a hard reliability constraint for agent responses. WebSocket requires reconnection handling for long-running bots. Offset pagination. Partial idempotency — message sends not idempotent. Mattermost Cloud option provides managed reliability.

af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server for personal stock analysis (quality 80). Requires TIINGO_API_KEY (mandatory). Optional keys for OpenRouter, Exa, OpenAI, Anthropic, FRED, Tavily — multiple keys needed for full functionality. Provides 39+ financial tools: technical indicators (SMA, EMA, RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands), VectorBT backtesting with 15+ strategies, stock screening, portfolio optimization. Requires TA-Lib system library installation (not just pip). Python 3.12+ required. SSE endpoint URL requires trailing slash. Pre-seeded with S&P 500 stocks only. Good for personal Claude Desktop stock analysis workflow.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local tool accessing external APIs (TIINGO, OKX) over HTTPS. Multiple API keys required — complex key management. No scope granularity on individual keys. Community maintained — dependency hygiene depends on maintainer. Multiple dependency chains: akshare, VectorBT, TA-Lib add supply chain risk. API keys via environment variables. No compliance certifications. Financial data is read-only — limited blast radius. Analysis results are local — no data exfiltration risk from the tool itself.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool — no SLA. Depends on TIINGO and other external API availability. Unknown version — community maintained. No formal breaking change policy. Idempotency: yes (data queries are deterministic). A-share data only available during Chinese trading hours for akshare-sourced data. TA-Lib system library adds installation complexity. Multiple API keys create multiple potential failure points. Not suitable for unattended production financial automation.

af_score_components.notes: IMPORTANT: MaxKB is an MCP CLIENT platform, not an MCP server — it consumes MCP tools rather than exposing them. No MCP server capability. Open-source enterprise RAG + agent platform (Django/Vue). Default admin credentials are insecure (admin/MaxKB@123.) — must change immediately. Documentation and UI primarily in Chinese. Requires PostgreSQL with pgvector extension. GPLv3 license has copyleft implications. Supports multiple LLM backends including DeepSeek, Llama, Qwen, OpenAI, Claude, Gemini. Good for enterprise self-hosted knowledge base chatbots but not suitable for agent-to-agent integration without custom REST API work.

security_score_components.security_notes: CRITICAL: Default admin credentials (admin/MaxKB@123.) are publicly known and must be changed immediately. TLS depends entirely on deployment — not enforced by default Docker setup. No documented scope or RBAC controls. Open source Django project — dependency hygiene depends on maintainer. GPLv3 license. PostgreSQL credentials are deployment responsibility. LLM API keys stored in application config. Security score of 0 in existing evaluation reflects fundamental gaps. Not suitable for production without significant hardening.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Unknown version — newer project. No formal breaking change policy. Unknown idempotency behavior. No error recovery documentation. Requires PostgreSQL with pgvector extension — infrastructure dependency. Primary documentation and community is Chinese-language — English support limited. Early-stage adoption means significant rough edges. Not suitable for production agent workflows without significant investment in hardening and documentation.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. MaxMind GeoIP2 industry-standard IP geolocation and minFraud fraud scoring. HTTP Basic Auth (Account ID + License Key). GeoLite2 downloadable database files (MMDB format) for zero-latency offline lookups — must re-download twice weekly. License key for GeoLite2 is different from GeoIP2 web service key. MMDB format requires MaxMind's reader library. GeoLite2 accuracy significantly lower than paid GeoIP2 for city-level data. Retry guidance documented. Full idempotency on all GET queries. Good for fraud detection and geo-blocking agents. GDPR compliant.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. GDPR compliant. HTTP Basic Auth with Account ID + License Key. Separate license keys for GeoLite2 vs GeoIP2 web service provide feature-level isolation. No per-operation scope granularity. MaxMind (Neustar/TransUnion subsidiary) maintained — established company. License key via environment variable. IP address data is PII in some jurisdictions — appropriate data governance required. GeoLite2 GDPR attribution requirement applies per license terms. Read-only intelligence — no write operations minimize blast radius.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS web service with SLA. Stable versioned API with good backward compatibility. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Full idempotency for all GET queries. GeoLite2 database updated twice weekly — agents using local files must automate update workflow. Database file format (MMDB) is stable long-term. Web service and offline database options provide redundancy. GeoLite2 has no web API — offline DB only. Excellent overall reliability for IP intelligence automation.

af_score_components.notes: Official AWS Labs MCP server collection (quality 80) — 12+ individual servers covering AWS docs, CloudFormation, CDK, Terraform, EKS, ECS, SAM CLI, Lambda, Cloud Control API, and AWS Support. AWS credentials via profiles or environment variables. SSE transport was removed — stdio only, which limits remote deployment patterns. Infrastructure modification servers can make real AWS changes with real cost implications. Some servers require specific IAM policies. Uses uvx for installation requiring Python environment. Official backing from AWS Labs means good maintenance and compatibility. Excellent for AWS-heavy AI coding workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: AWS services enforce HTTPS. AWS IAM with fine-grained policies — per-service, per-action, per-resource scoping is gold standard for least-privilege. SOC2/HIPAA/FedRAMP compliance from AWS. Official AWS Labs — rigorous security review and maintenance. AWS credentials via environment variables or IAM roles (preferred — no static credentials). Infrastructure modification servers have high financial and operational blast radius — create scoped IAM policies. IAM roles for EC2/Lambda environments avoid static credential management.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: AWS services have SLA — underlying reliability is excellent. Local stdio servers have no SLA. 12+ servers with different capabilities adds operational complexity. No retry guidance documented. Partial idempotency — CloudFormation/CDK deployments idempotent by design; other operations may not be. SSE transport removal limits deployment flexibility. Infrastructure modifications have real AWS consequences — agents must handle errors carefully. AWS API rate limits (per-service) require per-server planning. Active AWS Labs maintenance provides good version stability.

af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server for Chinese stock and crypto market data via akshare (quality 65). No authentication required. Supports A-share, Hong Kong, and US stock markets plus OKX/Binance crypto. A-share market data only available during Chinese trading hours. Documentation and tool descriptions primarily in Chinese. Relies on akshare upstream which may have rate limits or unexpected breakage. Crypto endpoints depend on OKX/Binance availability. No retry guidance. Optional proxy configuration via environment variables. Good niche tool for Chinese market agents using Claude Desktop. Read-only data queries — no write operations.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local MCP server — no network exposure for the server itself. Connects to external APIs (akshare, OKX, Binance) over HTTPS. No auth required for the MCP server. Read-only financial data — minimal blast radius. Community maintained — akshare upstream dependency adds supply chain risk. No credentials stored. Optional proxy env vars for custom endpoints. No compliance certifications. Community project with unknown security review history. Data fetched is public market data — no PII concerns.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool — no SLA. Version 0.1.14 — early stage. Depends on akshare upstream reliability (community maintained). Crypto endpoints depend on OKX/Binance API availability. A-share data unavailable outside Chinese trading hours. No retry guidance documented. Idempotency: yes for data queries (read-only, deterministic). Akshare data source changes can silently break tool functionality. Multiple external dependencies create reliability risk. Not suitable for production financial automation requiring guaranteed uptime.

af_score_components.notes: OpenAI API to MCP bridge middleware (quality 60 as MCP-aware REST server). Optional API key auth (disabled by default — unauthenticated by default). Bridges OpenAI function calling format to MCP tool calls. Requires separate inference engine with tool-call support (vLLM or Ollama). Tool call quality depends on inference engine function calling capabilities. SSE bridge adds latency vs direct MCP connections. Configuration via JSON file for MCP server definitions (not env vars). Version 0.5.1. Streaming and non-streaming chat completions supported. Good for OpenAI SDK users wanting MCP tools without rewriting client code.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted — TLS depends on deployment. CRITICAL: API key authentication is DISABLED BY DEFAULT — local network exposure is unauthenticated without explicit configuration. No scope control — auth is binary enabled/disabled. Community maintained — FastAPI based. No compliance certifications. API key via config when enabled. MCP server credentials stored in JSON config file — secure file permissions required. Security posture depends heavily on deployment environment and network access controls.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Version 0.5.1 — moderately stable. No formal breaking change policy. Unknown idempotency. Adds middleware layer with additional latency and failure modes. Requires compatible inference engine running separately. No retry guidance documented. Tool call quality is inference-engine-dependent — non-deterministic reliability. SSE bridge adds complexity for stateful connections. Not suitable for production without hardening.

af_score_components.notes: NOT an MCP server — this is a Chinese-language tutorial and documentation repository for learning MCP development. Cannot be deployed or integrated into agent workflows as a callable service. Covers MCP server development with Python/FastMCP, client implementation, tool integration, sampling callbacks, LLM integration (DeepSeek), and Claude Desktop configuration. Scores reflect that this is reference documentation rather than an executable API or tool. For human developer education only. Use for learning MCP concepts; do not attempt to integrate as a service.

security_score_components.security_notes: Not a deployable service — security assessment not directly applicable. Static documentation repository on GitHub. Code examples are for learning and may not follow production security best practices. Content in Simplified Chinese only. No network operations, no credentials, no API surface. Security score reflects that this is not a deployable service but contains example code that may demonstrate API key handling patterns.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Not a deployable service — reliability assessment not applicable. Static GitHub documentation repository. Content may become outdated as MCP spec and FastMCP evolve. Unknown version. No formal maintenance policy. Useful exclusively as a learning resource for Chinese-speaking MCP developers. Code examples may not reflect latest MCP API changes.

af_score_components.notes: IMPORTANT: ollmcp is an MCP CLIENT (terminal TUI), not an MCP server — it consumes MCP servers, it does not expose MCP capabilities. No auth for the client itself. Connects local Ollama models to any MCP server for tool use. Agent mode with iterative tool execution — agent loop limit should be configured to prevent runaway execution. Tool call quality depends on Ollama model's function calling ability. Human-in-the-loop approval for sensitive tool executions. Supports STDIO/SSE/Streamable HTTP transports. Requires Ollama running locally. Good for testing MCP servers and local agent workflows. Not an agent-integratable service.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local TUI tool — no network exposure for the client itself. Connects to MCP servers and Ollama over local or network connections. No credentials stored in the client — auth delegated to each MCP server. Human-in-the-loop approval is a security feature for sensitive tool calls. Community maintained — relatively new project. No compliance certifications. Tool execution scope depends on connected MCP servers, not this client. Agent loop without limit could exhaust resources or cause repeated tool calls.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local TUI tool — no SLA. Unknown version. No formal breaking change policy. Requires Ollama running locally or via Ollama Cloud. Unknown idempotency — depends on connected MCP server behavior. Agent mode loop limit must be configured to prevent runaway execution. Conversation history export/import provides some continuity. Not suitable for production autonomous agent deployments requiring guaranteed uptime. Good for developer testing and supervised local workflows.
